exampleSentences: Anh tưởng em đã về nhà rồi nên anh không gọi.
Chị tưởng hôm nay là thứ bảy nên đi làm muộn.
Em tưởng cửa hàng này mở đến tối nhưng nó đóng cửa sớm.
notes:
tưởng = to think (but wrongly). You believed something was true, but it wasn’t.

Used when your assumption turns out incorrect. Very often followed by a full clause.  

Often appears with đã for past assumptions and with cứ tưởng to show stronger mistaken belief.  
Close to nghĩ (think), but tưởng implies you were wrong. Opposite idea: biết (know for sure).

translation.en: I thought that you would come.

notes_md:
**tưởng** = to think (but wrongly). You believed something was true, but it wasn’t.

Used when your assumption turns out incorrect. Very often followed by a full clause.  

Often appears with **đã** for past assumptions and with **cứ tưởng** to show stronger mistaken belief.  
Close to **nghĩ** (think), but **tưởng** implies you were wrong. Opposite idea: **biết** (know for sure).

translation.en: I thought that you would come.

definitions.en: To think or believe something, often based on an assumption.
definitions.es: Pensar o creer algo, a menudo basado en una suposición.
definitions.vi: Nghĩ hoặc tin điều gì đó, thường dựa trên một giả định.

example.vi: Tôi tưởng rằng bạn sẽ đến.

exampleSentencesTranslated: I think mistakenly that you already went home so I did not call.
I think mistakenly that today is Saturday so I went to work late.
I think mistakenly that this shop opens until evening but it closed early.
